author: Karta Ram, Master
title: Rahnuma-i-Tarjama-i-Angrezi, Part II
gloss: A guide to translation from Urdu into English for the use of students of the Middle and High Classes.
publisher: M. Hira Lal and Sons, Book-sellers
copyright: Lala Hira Lal, Hoshiárpur; No. 197, dated the 12th December, 1910.
notes: Section carries '(Designed for educational purposes.)'. Translation-drill guide at 3,000 copies, bookseller-copyrighted — English acquisition as the paying end of the pedagogy market.

title: Arsh Granthawali, Gita Hamen kaya Sikhati hai
gloss: The Arsh Granthawali Series, Volume VI, No. 6, for June, 1910, 'What does Gita teach us,' or some of the teachings of the Gít. Edited by Pandit Raja Ram.
notes: Arya Samaj subscription serial (Arsh Granthawali) registered issue-by-issue as 'books' — periodical print entering the registry sideways; bracket note: previous issue noticed in entry No. 15 at page 51 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th September, 1910 (our Q3 extraction).

title: Arsh Granthawali, Sabhashaya Arya Panch Maha Yogna Padhiti
gloss: The Arsh Granthawali Series, Volume VI, No. 7, for July, 1910, or the mantras of the five great Yajnas, with Hindi translation and the method of reciting them in the light of the Arya Samaj tenets, edited by Pandit Raja Ram.
notes: Dash-continuation of s17; ritual manual issue — yajna procedure 'in the light of Arya Samaj tenets'.

title: Arsh Granthawali Sabhashaya Sawadhayaya Yajna Rigvedake ke kai Sukton ka Bhashya
gloss: The Arsh Granthawali Series, Volume VI, No. 8, for August, 1910, or some mantras of the Rigveda, with Hindi translation and an independent commentary by Pandit Raja Ram.
notes: Dash-continuation of s18; three issues of the series registered across the quarter (regs 1140, 1143, 1290).

title: Ath 4 Chaubisi Puja
gloss: A collection of prayer, and methods of worship of Jain Saints, some in Hindi and some in Sanskrit.
notes: Jain liturgical compendium — 584 pp. at Rs. 10, the most expensive book of the quarter so far; Jain devotional economy: small community, monumental format (cf. the Jain apologetics at p29 s82).

flags.issue: printed serial 4 interrupts the section sequence (17, 18, 19, [4], 20) — possibly a misprint or an omitted Jain subsection header; transcribed as printed

title: Rigved Sanhita, Anks 47-48
gloss: The text of the Rigveda, with Hindi translation and an independent Hindi commentary, compiled by Rai Bahadur Sheo Nath.
notes: Fascicles 47–48 of a running Rigveda translation (pagination 2089–2167!) by a Rai Bahadur at Multan Cantonment — scripture publishing as multi-year serial infrastructure; the Veda reaching Hindi readers 80 pages per quarter.
